tossups.question: A Maysles documentary describes a rancher's experience sleeping beside an artwork by this man. Cologne Harbor was the site of an early artwork by this man titled Dockside Packages. For decades, this man tried to gain permission from the United Arab Emirates to build the stacked-oil-barrel sculpture Mastaba. This man created several large, inflatable indoor installations known as Air Packages. He used saffron-colored fabric to construct 7,503 structures in Central Park in his project The Gates, which, like virtually all of his work, was performed in collaboration with his Moroccan-born French wife Jean-Claude. For 10 points, name this Bulgarian mononymic artist who is best known for his part in wrapping the Reichstag.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Christo Vladimirov Javacheff [or Christo Vladimirov Javacheff]
tossups.question_sanitized: A Maysles documentary describes a rancher's experience sleeping beside an artwork by this man. Cologne Harbor was the site of an early artwork by this man titled Dockside Packages. For decades, this man tried to gain permission from the United Arab Emirates to build the stacked-oil-barrel sculpture Mastaba. This man created several large, inflatable indoor installations known as Air Packages. He used saffron-colored fabric to construct 7,503 structures in Central Park in his project The Gates, which, like virtually all of his work, was performed in collaboration with his Moroccan-born French wife Jean-Claude. For 10 points, name this Bulgarian mononymic artist who is best known for his part in wrapping the Reichstag.

bonuses.leadin: For an exhibit running from June to October 2016, an artist installed one of these features outside the Palace of Versailles's Grand Canal. For 10 points each:
answers: Christo Vladimirov Javacheff AND Jeanne-Claude Denat de Guillebon [prompt on partial answers]
parts: In 2008, the Danish-Icelandic artist Olafur Eliasson ("oh-lah-foor el-EE-ah-son") recreated what sort of natural feature underneath Brooklyn Bridge?
In 2008, Eliasson ("el-EE-ah-son") surpassed this couple's installations of orange gates in Central Park as the most-expensive public arts project. This couple also wrapped the Reichstag in plastic.

In 2018, Eliasson ("el-EE-ah-son") installed blocks of ice in this city to showcase the negative effects of climate change. Eliasson ("el-EE-ah-son") also created The Weather Project for this city's Tate Modern museum.
answers_sanitized: Christo Vladimirov Javacheff AND Jeanne-Claude Denat de Guillebon [prompt on partial answers]

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about when art kills, for 10 points each.
parts: During an installation of an Alexander Calder piece at Princeton, a snapped cable resulted in the death of two workmen. Calder originated this type of sculpture whose shapes move in response to touch or air currents.

This artist and his wife once took seven years to set up thousands of yellow umbrellas in California and blue umbrellas in Japan as part of an art project. High winds blew an umbrella in California loose, killing a bystander.

A piece by this sculptor, noted for working with large-scale assemblies of sheet metal, had a part break free from its support and kill a rigger in Minneapolis. He installed Tilted Arc, an arc of rusted steel in New York City's Federal Plaza.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about when art kills, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: During an installation of an Alexander Calder piece at Princeton, a snapped cable resulted in the death of two workmen. Calder originated this type of sculpture whose shapes move in response to touch or air currents.

This artist and his wife once took seven years to set up thousands of yellow umbrellas in California and blue umbrellas in Japan as part of an art project. High winds blew an umbrella in California loose, killing a bystander.

A piece by this sculptor, noted for working with large-scale assemblies of sheet metal, had a part break free from its support and kill a rigger in Minneapolis. He installed Tilted Arc, an arc of rusted steel in New York City's Federal Plaza.

bonuses.leadin: A series of yellow umbrellas in this state complemented a group of blue umbrellas half-way around the world in Asia. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this state where one can find Running Fence and the Winchester Mystery House.
Those umbrellas and Running Fence were artworks designed by Jean-Claude and this artist, who also wrapped a bunch of islands in nylon pink.
This other work of Christo consists of unfurled rolls of saffron colored fabric hanging from the namesake structure in Central Park. It was only on public display in 2005.
